speechOptions.instruction: Speak in a bright, warm, hopeful tone — like happily introducing something you love to a friend. Gentle, positive energy throughout; never dark or flat.

title: Turn Routine Work into Buttons
description: A new MulmoClaude collections feature, demonstrated live: two kinds of buttons you can put on a record's page — an agent action where the AI looks up the latest stock price behind the scenes, and a mutate action that flips an invoice to Paid instantly with no AI. Both built just by asking.

beats.text: Hello! Today I'd like to show you buttons — a recent addition to MulmoClaude collections that you can put right on a data page. Everyday operations like refreshing a stock price or recording a payment now finish with a single press.

html: <div class='t-bo relative w-[1280px] h-[720px] overflow-hidden' style='background:radial-gradient(1200px 700px at 12% -10%,rgba(253,224,71,.30),transparent 60%),radial-gradient(1000px 600px at 100% 0%,rgba(251,146,60,.22),transparent 55%),linear-gradient(160deg,#FFFBF2,#FFF3DC 55%,#FCE8C8);color:#431407'>
New features
Everyday operations are now buttons
Refresh a stock price or record a payment — one press, right on the page
💬 One press, and the AI writes in the latest price

beats.text: There are two kinds: a button where the AI works behind the scenes, and a button that doesn't use AI at all. Both are made just by asking. Let's build them and see.

html: <div class='t-bo relative w-[1280px] h-[720px] overflow-hidden' style='background:radial-gradient(1200px 700px at 12% -10%,rgba(253,224,71,.30),transparent 60%),radial-gradient(1000px 600px at 100% 0%,rgba(251,146,60,.22),transparent 55%),linear-gradient(160deg,#FFFBF2,#FFF3DC 55%,#FCE8C8);color:#431407'>
<div class='flex flex-col items-center justify-center h-full text-center px-16'>
NEW IN MULMOCLAUDE COLLECTIONS

Turn routine work
into buttons
A button backed by AI · A button with no AI

beats.text: In the portfolio collection, we ask: add a Refresh Price button to each stock's page, and when it's pressed, have the AI look up the latest price and update the record. Words alone — and the button is born.

html: <div class='t-bo relative w-[1280px] h-[720px] overflow-hidden' style='background:radial-gradient(1200px 700px at 12% -10%,rgba(253,224,71,.30),transparent 60%),radial-gradient(1000px 600px at 100% 0%,rgba(251,146,60,.22),transparent 55%),linear-gradient(160deg,#FFFBF2,#FFF3DC 55%,#FCE8C8);color:#431407'>
STEP 1
Ask for the AI button
One sentence — "add a Refresh Price button" — and it exists
💬 "Add a Refresh Price button to each stock's page — the AI should look up the latest share price and update the record"

beats.text: Press the button, and the AI quietly goes to work behind the scenes. No chat window opens. A little later, the price and market value had changed to the latest figures. This is an agent action.

html: <div class='t-bo relative w-[1280px] h-[720px] overflow-hidden' style='background:radial-gradient(1200px 700px at 12% -10%,rgba(253,224,71,.30),transparent 60%),radial-gradient(1000px 600px at 100% 0%,rgba(251,146,60,.22),transparent 55%),linear-gradient(160deg,#FFFBF2,#FFF3DC 55%,#FCE8C8);color:#431407'>
STEP 1
Press it, and the AI works backstage
No chat opens. Next time you look, the price is current
<div class='flex items-center justify-center gap-4 mt-16 px-8'>
Just press "Refresh Price"
→
<div class='relative' data-animation='animate' data-easing='easeOut' data-opacity='0,1' data-sync='2:0.15' data-start='7.11' data-end='7.61'>
The AI wrote in the latest price

beats.text: The other kind is a button that doesn't use AI. This time, we ask like this. For routine changes, there's no need to bring the AI in at all.

html: <div class='t-bo relative w-[1280px] h-[720px] overflow-hidden' style='background:radial-gradient(1200px 700px at 12% -10%,rgba(253,224,71,.30),transparent 60%),radial-gradient(1000px 600px at 100% 0%,rgba(251,146,60,.22),transparent 55%),linear-gradient(160deg,#FFFBF2,#FFF3DC 55%,#FCE8C8);color:#431407'>
STEP 2
Ask for the plain button
For routine changes, a button with no AI is plenty

💬 "Add a Mark as Paid button to invoice pages. No AI involved — pressing it should just flip the status instantly.
Only show it on invoices that have already been sent"

beats.text: When a payment arrives, one press. No judgment and no AI needed — zero wait, and the status flips to Paid on the spot. This is a mutate action.

html: <div class='t-bo relative w-[1280px] h-[720px] overflow-hidden' style='background:radial-gradient(1200px 700px at 12% -10%,rgba(253,224,71,.30),transparent 60%),radial-gradient(1000px 600px at 100% 0%,rgba(251,146,60,.22),transparent 55%),linear-gradient(160deg,#FFFBF2,#FFF3DC 55%,#FCE8C8);color:#431407'>
STEP 2
Press it, and it's done
Zero wait — the status flips to "Paid" on the spot
<div class='flex items-center justify-center gap-4 mt-16 px-8'>
Just press "Mark as Paid"
→
<div class='relative' data-animation='animate' data-easing='easeOut' data-opacity='0,1' data-sync='1:0.45' data-start='5.72' data-end='6.22'>
Instantly "Paid"

beats.text: Choosing between them is simple. Work that needs looking things up or judgment: the AI button. Routine, fixed changes: the plain button. And either one can be added later, in conversation, the moment you want it.

html: <div class='t-bo relative w-[1280px] h-[720px] overflow-hidden' style='background:radial-gradient(1200px 700px at 12% -10%,rgba(253,224,71,.30),transparent 60%),radial-gradient(1000px 600px at 100% 0%,rgba(251,146,60,.22),transparent 55%),linear-gradient(160deg,#FFFBF2,#FFF3DC 55%,#FCE8C8);color:#431407'>
Which one?
Two kinds of buttons — which to ask for?
When in doubt: does it need the AI's judgment?
<div class='flex items-stretch justify-center gap-8 mt-12 px-16'>

<div class='flex-1 rounded-2xl px-10 py-10 text-center' data-animation='animate' data-easing='easeOut' data-opacity='0,1' data-translate-y='10,0' data-sync='1:0.0' data-start='2.55' data-end='3.05' style='background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #EAD9BE;box-shadow:0 10px 24px rgba(67,20,7,.08);border:2px solid #EA580C'>

Looks things up, weighs them
e.g. "Refresh Price"

<div class='flex-1 rounded-2xl px-10 py-10 text-center' data-animation='animate' data-easing='easeOut' data-opacity='0,1' data-translate-y='10,0' data-sync='2:0.0' data-start='6.41' data-end='6.91' style='background:#FFFFFF;border:1px solid #EAD9BE;box-shadow:0 10px 24px rgba(67,20,7,.08);border:2px solid #0284C7'>

Fixed, routine changes
e.g. "Mark as Paid"

beats.text: Your everyday operations start finishing without ever opening a chat. A collection grows to fit the way you work — one button at a time. Pick one thing you often ask for, and make it a button.

html: <div class='t-bo relative w-[1280px] h-[720px] overflow-hidden' style='background:radial-gradient(1200px 700px at 12% -10%,rgba(253,224,71,.30),transparent 60%),radial-gradient(1000px 600px at 100% 0%,rgba(251,146,60,.22),transparent 55%),linear-gradient(160deg,#FFFBF2,#FFF3DC 55%,#FCE8C8);color:#431407'>
<div class='flex flex-col items-center justify-center h-full text-center px-20'>

Everyday operations,
done without opening a chat

Pick one operation you often ask for, and make it a button.
Just say, "add a button that does ..." — in conversation.
🌱 Collections keep growing after they're built — give it a try!
